title: Spencerian minuscule — the oval group
chartDefinition: calligraphyExemplar — Spencerian minuscule, letters o, a, d, g, q shown with oval construction, nib angle near-horizontal (5–7° from baseline), 3:2 shade rule
sources: Platt Rogers Spencer, Spencerian Key to Practical Penmanship (1866) — the oval principles and the minuscule alphabet
notes: The Spencerian alphabet builds from the oval — a slanted ellipse at 52° that forms the basis of a, d, g, o, q, p, b and the compound curves of m, n, h. The pointed-pen shade falls on the down stroke only; hairlines return on the up. Nib angle nearly horizontal compared with broad-edge traditions.
